title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers multi-tiered caching on top of cloud object storage like AWS S3, combining cost-effective scalability with local NVMe performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, local SSD caching, and monitoring cache performance via Prometheus integration.
writeup: The video demonstrates JuiceFS, a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that mounts cloud object storage such as AWS S3 as a local drive while using local NVMe as a cache tier. The core design separates data (stored in object storage) from metadata (managed by a store like Redis), and a multi-tiered cache aims to deliver local-disk performance alongside cloud scalability. The walkthrough covers practical setup with Redis for metadata, monitoring through Prometheus, and testing against real inference workloads. It matters because it shows a concrete path to large, scalable storage with familiar file semantics without provisioning a multi-petabyte local array.
